name: Mirador de Rabasa, Cueva de Lagrimas – Ruta Circular – Sierra Salinas
region_intro: Mountain biking around Yecla traverses a diverse landscape in the Murcia region of Spain. The terrain features numerous mountains and ranges, including Monte Arabí, Sierra del Carche, and Sierra Salinas, offering varied paths from gentle routes to challenging mountain trails. The region is characterized by high plains and steppes adorned with vineyards, almond trees, and olive groves, providing scenic backdrops for cycling. Ravines and natural springs also contribute to the varied natural features encountered on the routes. Best mountain bike trails around Yecla The most popular mountain bike trail is Yecla, Monte Arabí, Cueva de La Horadad – Ruta Circular, a 32.0 miles (51.6 km) moderate trail that takes 3 hours 59 minutes to complete. This route explores the unique rock formations and natural features of Monte Arabí. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Mirador de Rabasa, Cueva de Lagrimas – Ruta Circular – Sierra Salinas, a moderate 9.5 miles (15.3 km) path. This route offers viewpoints and traverses parts of the Sierra Salinas mountain range. Local mountain bikers also love the Parking Area – Raspay Village loop from Raspay, a 25.3 miles (40.8 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 58 minutes. Mountain biking around Yecla is defined by its mountain ranges, agricultural steppes, and ravines. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to difficult ascents. The routes in Yecla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 700 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Yecla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Yecla?
A: There are over 90 mountain bike trails around Yecla, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. The komoot community has explored more than 700 mountain biking tours in the area.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Yecla?
A: Yecla's mountain bike trails traverse a diverse landscape. You'll find paths through high plains and steppes adorned with vineyards, almond, and olive groves, as well as challenging routes through mountains and ranges like Monte Arabí, Sierra del Carche, and Sierra Salinas. The terrain also features ravines, natural springs, and unique rock formations.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Yecla?
A: Yes, Yecla offers several mountain bike trails suitable for beginners. There are 7 easy routes available, perfect for those new to mountain biking or looking for a more relaxed ride. These typically involve less elevation gain and shorter distances.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes in Yecla for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Yecla has 20 difficult mountain bike routes. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14630667" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parking Area – Raspay Village loop from Raspay&lt;/a&gt;, a 25.3-mile (40.8 km) trail with significant elevation changes, often completed in about 3 hours 58 minutes. This route leads through varied and demanding terrain.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Yecla?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Yecla are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e933565314/yecla-monte-arabi-cueva-de-la-horadad-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yecla, Monte Arabí, Cueva de La Horadad – Ruta Circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 32.0-mile (51.6 km) moderate trail exploring the unique features of Monte Arabí.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while mountain biking in Yecla?
A: While mountain biking in Yecla, you can encounter a variety of natural features and landmarks. These include the unique rock formations of Monte Arabí, prehistoric rock art in caves like Canto de la Visera and Mediodía, and the highest point in the municipality at Sierra Salinas. You might also pass by significant caves such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3933197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Horadada Cave of Monte Arabí&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4264671" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva del Lagrimal&lt;/a&gt;, known for its dripping water and views.

Q: Can I visit Monte Arabí on a mountain bike trail?
A: Absolutely. Monte Arabí, known as the 'Magic Mountain' with its unique rock formations and prehistoric rock art, is a central highlight for mountain biking. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e933565314/yecla-monte-arabi-cueva-de-la-horadad-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yecla, Monte Arabí, Cueva de La Horadad – Ruta Circular&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example of a route that explores this significant natural feature.

Q: What are the highlights of mountain biking in the Sierra Salinas area?
A: Mountain biking in the Sierra Salinas area offers routes through one of the most important forest ecosystems in the Altiplano region, including the highest point in Yecla. Trails here provide scenic views and opportunities to explore pine-covered hills. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e930929374/mirador-de-rabasa-cueva-de-lagrimas-ruta-circular-sierra-salinas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Rabasa, Cueva de Lagrimas – Ruta Circular – Sierra Salinas&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that showcases this beautiful mountain range.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the mountain bike trails in Yecla?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Yecla offer fantastic viewpoints. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e930929374/mirador-de-rabasa-cueva-de-lagrimas-ruta-circular-sierra-salinas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Rabasa, Cueva de Lagrimas – Ruta Circular – Sierra Salinas&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights a viewpoint. Monte Arabí also provides fabulous views of the surrounding countryside from its elevated positions.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Yecla?
A: The mountain bike trails in Yecla are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 30 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, ranging from agricultural steppes to challenging mountain ranges, and the scenic beauty of the region, including its unique rock formations and pine forests.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Yecla?
A: Yecla's mild climate makes it suitable for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for outdoor activities, avoiding the peak heat of summer and potential colder, wetter conditions in winter. This allows for comfortable exploration of the diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike routes in Yecla?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' routes are not explicitly detailed, the region's diverse terrain includes easier paths that could be suitable for families. Additionally, the 'Chicharra' greenway, an old railway track bed extending towards Alicante, provides a flat and gentle option perfect for family bike rides.
tips: Rest for a while and admire the spectacular views.

A large cave (330 m2 of surface) and very visited. The water drips from the roof, especially in rainy seasons, to which it owes its name. It presents archaeological remains from the Mesolithic to the Arab period. The water is used by various species (for example, birds, mammals, bats). It is located in a very beautiful corner, with holm oaks, gall oaks, Aleppo pines and dense laurel bushes.
